methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Vermeer is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparables universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private-stage companies in the same sector, including Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the company's $10M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding an implied value of $20M. The peer-median method examines 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables. The peer set ranges from $125M to $3.0B in valuation, with a median of $1.0B, reflecting significant maturity dispersion within the defense-tech cohort.

These two methods generate a wide triangulated range: a low of $17M, a median of $510M, and a high of $1.1B. The substantial spread reflects tension between the early-stage revenue/funding proxy ($20M) and the peer universe median ($1.0B), which is skewed toward later-stage Series B valuations. Key uncertainties include Vermeer's relative product maturity, go-to-market traction, and technical differentiation against the named peer set. A valuation in the $50M–$150M range would position the company conservatively within its stage while acknowledging sector tailwinds, but this requires validation against growth metrics not present in the provided data.
